subject: FAR
title: Not-for-Profit Contributions and Net Asset Classification
scenario:
Harlow University, a private not-for-profit university, is preparing its statement of activities for the year ended December 31, Year 2.

Contributions received or pledged during Year 2 are summarized in **Exhibit 1**. Information about donor-restricted net assets, including amounts spent in accordance with donor stipulations, appears in **Exhibit 2**. Other operating information appears in **Exhibit 3**.

Harlow's accounting policy is to report gifts of long-lived assets that carry no donor stipulations as support without donor restrictions when the assets are placed in service (no time restriction is implied). Use the information in the exhibits to complete the tasks that follow.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 1: Contributions Received or Pledged During Year 2
exhibits.content:
| # | Description | Amount |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Cash contributions from annual giving campaign, no donor stipulations | $500,000 |
| 2 | Cash gift from an alumna, restricted by the donor for the purchase of laboratory equipment | $200,000 |
| 3 | Cash gift restricted by the donor for use in Year 3 operations | $150,000 |
| 4 | Cash gift to establish a permanent endowment; the corpus must be maintained in perpetuity, and investment income is restricted by the donor for scholarships | $1,000,000 |
| 5 | Unconditional promise to give (pledge) received in December Year 2, due in January Year 3, deemed fully collectible | $120,000 |
| 6 | Conditional promise to give: a donor promised to contribute $300,000 contingent on the university raising an equal amount in new contributions from other donors during Year 3. The matching campaign will not begin until January Year 3, and no cash has been received from this donor. | $300,000 |
| 7 | Audit services donated by a CPA firm. The university would otherwise have purchased these services. | Fair value $40,000 |
| 8 | Volunteer services for the annual phone-a-thon and campus tours, provided by volunteers with no specialized skills | Estimated value $25,000 |
| 9 | Donated computer equipment with no donor stipulations, placed in service during Year 2 | Fair value $60,000 |

exhibits.title: Exhibit 2: Donor-Restricted Net Assets — Selected Information
exhibits.content:
- Net assets with donor restrictions at January 1, Year 2 were **$800,000**. This balance includes a $90,000 gift received in Year 1 that the donor restricted for use in Year 2 operations.
- During Year 2, the university spent **$160,000** of the $200,000 laboratory equipment gift (Exhibit 1, item 2) on qualifying equipment purchases. The remainder will be spent in Year 3.
- The **$90,000** Year 1 time-restricted gift was used for Year 2 operations as the donor stipulated.
- Investment income earned in Year 2 on the permanent endowment, restricted by the donor for scholarships, was **$50,000**.
- Scholarships of **$30,000** were awarded and paid during Year 2 from restricted endowment income.

exhibits.title: Exhibit 3: Other Operating Information for Year 2
exhibits.content:
| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Tuition and fees revenue (net of financial aid) | $2,400,000 |
| Auxiliary enterprises revenue (dining and housing) | $380,000 |
| Interest income on operating cash, no donor restrictions | $12,000 |

In December Year 2, the board of directors voted to designate $250,000 of net assets without donor restrictions as a quasi-endowment for future campus improvements.

tasks.title: Classification of Contributions
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibit 1. Select all items that Harlow should report as contribution revenue (support) with donor restrictions on its Year 2 statement of activities.
options: Cash gift of $200,000 restricted for the purchase of laboratory equipment
Cash gift of $150,000 restricted for use in Year 3 operations
Cash gift of $1,000,000 to establish a permanent endowment
Unconditional promise to give of $120,000 due in January Year 3
Cash contributions of $500,000 with no donor stipulations
correctAnswer: Cash gift of $200,000 restricted for the purchase of laboratory equipment
Cash gift of $150,000 restricted for use in Year 3 operations
Cash gift of $1,000,000 to establish a permanent endowment
Unconditional promise to give of $120,000 due in January Year 3
tasks.explanation: Purpose-restricted gifts ($200,000 for equipment), time-restricted gifts ($150,000 for Year 3), and perpetual endowment gifts ($1,000,000) are all reported as support with donor restrictions. An unconditional promise to give that is due in a future period ($120,000 due January Year 3) is recognized in the period pledged and carries an implied time restriction, so it is also classified as with donor restrictions. The $500,000 with no donor stipulations is support without donor restrictions. The $300,000 conditional promise is not recognized at all until the matching condition is substantially met.
tasks.explanationJa:
純資産の2区分（with / without donor restrictions）への分類問題です。

**with donor restrictions（制限あり）に分類されるもの:**

| 項目 | 理由 |
|---|---|
| 実験機器目的の $200,000 | 使途制限（purpose restriction） |
| Year 3 使用目的の $150,000 | 期間制限（time restriction） |
| 永久基金 $1,000,000 | 永久制限（perpetual in nature） |
| 翌期回収予定の誓約 $120,000 | 将来期日到来のpledgeには**期間制限が黙示的に付される** |

**含まれないもの:**
- 無条件・無制限の現金寄付 $500,000 → without donor restrictions
- 条件付き寄付 $300,000 → **条件が実質的に充足されるまで一切認識しない**（分類以前の問題）

tasks.title: Contribution Revenue Without Donor Restrictions
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibit 1. Calculate total contribution revenue (support) without donor restrictions that Harlow should recognize for Year 2. Exclude net assets released from restrictions (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: Cash contributions with no donor stipulations of $500,000, donated audit services of $40,000 (recognized because they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would otherwise be purchased), and donated computer equipment of $60,000 (no donor stipulations; the university's policy does not imply a time restriction) are all support without donor restrictions. Total = $500,000 + $40,000 + $60,000 = $600,000. The $25,000 of general volunteer services does not meet the recognition criteria and is not recognized.
tasks.explanationJa:
without donor restrictions の寄付収益を集計します。

| 項目 | 金額 | 判定 |
|---|---|---|
| 無制限の現金寄付 | $500,000 | 認識 |
| 監査サービスの寄付 | $40,000 | 専門技能＋購入必要性を満たすため**認識** |
| コンピュータ機器の現物寄付 | $60,000 | 制限なし・時間制限を黙示しない方針のため認識 |
| 一般ボランティア | ($25,000) | 認識要件を満たさず**認識しない** |

合計 = $500,000 + $40,000 + $60,000 = **$600,000**

※制限解除額（released from restrictions）は寄付収益ではなく振替（reclassification）なので含めません。

tasks.title: Contribution Revenue With Donor Restrictions
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibit 1. Calculate total contribution revenue (support) with donor restrictions that Harlow should recognize for Year 2 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: Laboratory equipment gift $200,000 + gift for Year 3 operations $150,000 + permanent endowment gift $1,000,000 + unconditional pledge due January Year 3 $120,000 = $1,470,000. The $300,000 conditional promise is not recognized because the matching condition has not been met. The $50,000 of endowment investment income increases net assets with donor restrictions but is reported as investment return, not contribution revenue.
tasks.explanationJa:
with donor restrictions の寄付収益を集計します。

| 項目 | 金額 |
|---|---|
| 実験機器目的の寄付 | $200,000 |
| Year 3 使用目的の寄付 | $150,000 |
| 永久基金への寄付 | $1,000,000 |
| 翌期期日の無条件誓約 | $120,000 |
| **合計** | **$1,470,000** |

**含めないもの:**
- 条件付き寄付 $300,000: 条件（マッチング）未充足のため認識しない
- 基金運用収益 $50,000: 制限付き純資産を増加させるが、**投資収益**であり寄付収益ではない

tasks.title: Conditional Promise to Give
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibit 1, item 6. How should Harlow account for the $300,000 conditional promise to give in its Year 2 financial statements?
options: Recognize contribution revenue with donor restrictions of $300,000 in Year 2
Recognize a refundable advance (liability) of $300,000 in Year 2
Recognize no contribution revenue in Year 2; recognize revenue when the matching condition is substantially met
Recognize contribution revenue of $150,000, representing one-half of the conditional amount
tasks.correctAnswer: Recognize no contribution revenue in Year 2; recognize revenue when the matching condition is substantially met
tasks.explanation: A conditional promise to give — one that depends on the occurrence of a specified future and uncertain event, such as obtaining matching gifts — is recognized only when the condition is substantially met. Because the matching campaign has not begun and no cash has been received, Harlow recognizes nothing in Year 2 (footnote disclosure is appropriate). A refundable advance is recorded only when cash is received before the condition is met.
tasks.explanationJa:
条件付き寄付（conditional promise to give）の取扱いです。

- 条件付き寄付は、**条件が実質的に充足されるまで収益認識しない**（開示のみ）
- マッチング（同額の追加調達）は典型的な「障壁（barrier）」であり、Year 3 のキャンペーン開始前なので未充足
- **現金を先に受け取っていた場合**のみ、refundable advance（返還義務のある前受金＝負債）を計上する。本問では現金未受領のため負債計上も不要

したがって Year 2 では何も認識せず、条件充足時に収益認識します。

tasks.title: Recognition Criteria for Contributed Services
tasks.instruction: Under ASC 958-605, which criterion explains why Harlow recognizes the donated audit services but not the volunteer services?
options: Contributed services are recognized only if they create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated
Contributed services are recognized whenever their fair value can be reasonably estimated
Contributed services are recognized only if the provider is a licensed professional, regardless of whether the services would otherwise be purchased
Contributed services are never recognized as contribution revenue
tasks.correctAnswer: Contributed services are recognized only if they create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated
tasks.explanation: Contributed services are recognized only if (a) they create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not provided by donation. The CPA firm's audit services meet criterion (b), so $40,000 is recognized. The phone-a-thon and campus tour volunteers have no specialized skills, so their services are not recognized.
tasks.explanationJa:
サービス寄付（contributed services）の認識要件は次の**いずれか**です。

1. **非金融資産を創出または増価**させるサービス（例: 建物建設のボランティア労働）
2. **専門技能**を要し、その技能を持つ者が提供し、寄付がなければ**購入する必要がある**サービス

- CPA事務所の監査サービス（$40,000）→ 要件2を満たす → **認識**
- 電話勧誘・キャンパスツアーのボランティア（$25,000）→ 専門技能なし → **認識しない**

「公正価値が見積れれば認識できる」わけではない点に注意してください。

tasks.title: Net Assets Released From Restrictions
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibit 2. Calculate total net assets released from restrictions that Harlow should report for Year 2 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: Restrictions are released when the purpose is fulfilled or the time restriction expires: qualifying laboratory equipment purchases of $160,000 (purpose restriction satisfied for the amount spent), the $90,000 Year 1 gift used in Year 2 operations (time restriction expired), and $30,000 of scholarships awarded from restricted endowment income (purpose restriction satisfied). Total released = $160,000 + $90,000 + $30,000 = $280,000. The unspent $40,000 of the equipment gift remains restricted.
tasks.explanationJa:
制限解除（net assets released from restrictions）は、**使途の充足**または**期間の経過**により生じます。

| 項目 | 金額 | 解除理由 |
|---|---|---|
| 実験機器の購入支出 | $160,000 | 使途制限の充足（支出した分のみ） |
| Year 1 の期間制限付き寄付 | $90,000 | Year 2 到来により期間制限が消滅 |
| 制限付き運用収益からの奨学金支給 | $30,000 | 使途制限の充足 |
| **合計** | **$280,000** | |

機器寄付の未使用分 $40,000（= $200,000 − $160,000）は引き続き制限付き純資産に残ります。

tasks.title: Net Assets With Donor Restrictions at Year-End
tasks.instruction: Refer to Exhibits 1 and 2. Calculate net assets with donor restrictions at December 31, Year 2 (in whole dollars, no commas).
tasks.explanation: Beginning balance $800,000 + contribution revenue with donor restrictions $1,470,000 (Work Tab 3) + restricted endowment investment income $50,000 − net assets released from restrictions $280,000 (Work Tab 6) = $2,040,000. The board's $250,000 quasi-endowment designation is a board designation of net assets without donor restrictions and does not affect this balance.
tasks.explanationJa:
with donor restrictions 純資産のロールフォワードです。

| 項目 | 金額 |
|---|---|
| 期首残高 | $800,000 |
| ＋ 制限付き寄付収益（Work Tab 3） | $1,470,000 |
| ＋ 制限付き基金運用収益 | $50,000 |
| − 制限解除額（Work Tab 6） | ($280,000) |
| **期末残高** | **$2,040,000** |

※理事会指定の準基金 $250,000 は**理事会の自主的指定**にすぎず、without donor restrictions の内訳のまま。ドナーによる制限ではないため本残高には影響しません。
